title: Figma UI/UX Masterclass
subtitle: From rectangles to design systems — the modern designer's primary tool
description: A practical course on Figma as it's actually used in design teams in 2026: file structure, auto layout, components and variants, variables and tokens, prototyping, dev handoff, and the collaboration workflows that make Figma a team tool, not just a canvas. For designers shipping work and engineers reading designs.
audience: Designers (any level), product engineers reading Figma daily, anyone learning visual design with the dominant tool.
prerequisites: Visual taste (no formal design training needed)
outcomes: Navigate Figma fluently — layers, frames, files, libraries
Build with auto layout and components
Collaborate with engineers via dev mode
Maintain a design system that scales

modules.title: The Figma foundation
modules.objective: How Figma is structured and how to move through it.

modules.title: Auto Layout
modules.objective: Make designs flex like real interfaces.

modules.title: Prototyping, handoff, and collaboration
modules.objective: Take designs from canvas to shipped product.

lessons.title: Dev Mode and the engineer's view
Maintaining a design system over time
